symptom: .gitignore explicitly allowed .env.example while the pre-commit hook rejected every staged .env* path including .env.example
root_cause: Secret policy and developer-onboarding policy encoded different exceptions
what_worked: Use config/example.env and document copying it to an ignored local environment file

symptom: npm run setup failed with EOVERRIDE for postcss after the patched release was added as a direct dependency
root_cause: package.json retained a broad transitive override after npm install --save-dev added the same package with a different semver string
what_worked: Keep the patched PostCSS version as one direct devDependency and remove the redundant override
what_didnt: combining postcss ^8.5.10 override with direct postcss ^8.5.19

symptom: fresh npm install E404 '@stockshield/contracts@* is not in this registry' in rebased worktrees; main a695f17..192724f contained @stockshield in services/zero-adapter/* and services/procurement/src/email.* despite CURRENT_STATE claiming a full sweep
root_cause: during the origin/main merge the @stockshield->@continuim perl sweep edited the WORKING TREE but was never git add-ed; git commit --no-edit committed the stale merge INDEX; verification grep and npm run check both ran against the clean working tree, hiding the gap
what_worked: lane C's fresh-install report; staging the already-swept files + regenerated lock and committing (8ae16d1)
what_didnt: grep-verifying the sweep on the working tree instead of the index/commit
treating pre-commit check (also working-tree) as merge-commit verification

symptom: After the StockShield->Continuim rename (decision 0016) landed on main, npm install fails repo-wide: 404 '@stockshield/contracts@* is not in this registry'. Files merged in the rename window kept @stockshield: services/procurement/src/email.ts+email.test.ts (lane B, fixed) and all services/zero-adapter/** (lane A).
root_cause: The reconciliation @stockshield->@continuim sweep ran before lane-B B3 (email module) and lane-A zero-adapter merged, so those files kept @stockshield names/imports/deps while the workspace packages became @continuim.
what_worked: Lane B swept its own files to @continuim (email.ts/email.test.ts imports + email subject; AKASH.md image names); npm run check 29/29 on the current tree
what_didnt: clean npm install still 404s until lane A sweeps services/zero-adapter/package.json (name + @stockshield/contracts dep) and its src imports — single-writer, not lane B's to edit

symptom: closure note for the prior open entry: its lane-A half (services/zero-adapter @stockshield refs) was already fixed on main by 8ae16d1 before the entry landed — messages crossed
root_cause: same class as merge-sweep-committed-stale-index: an incomplete rename sweep that looked green due to stale state
what_worked: 8ae16d1 (PM staged the missed sweep + relinked lock); commit-level verification (git grep HEAD) adopted
